extraction.one_line_summary: VIVIANI Elia won the sprint after MØRKØV Michael delivered a textbook lead-out, holding center position through the final curve to box out BOUHANNI Nacer.
tactical_patterns: MØRKØV Michael controlled centerline through final curve preventing BOUHANNI Nacer from positioning, demonstrating geometric control over power positioning.
Sky stripped GC riders from front in final kilometers preemptively avoiding crash risk despite no wind conditions.
GARCÍA CORTINA Iván went wide early attempting disruption sprint but lacked teammates to sustain positioning through final 400m.

rider_signals.VIVIANI Elia: positive: Acknowledged pressure after two winless sprint stages, delivered when team expectations peaked showing mental resilience under scrutiny.
rider_signals.MØRKØV Michael: positive: Executed lead-out holding tight centerline geometry through curve, preventing faster rivals from optimal positioning despite their speed.
rider_signals.BOUHANNI Nacer: negative: Boxed against barriers in finale despite positioning attempt, suggesting spatial awareness deficit in technical finishes.
rider_signals.SAGAN Peter: neutral: Present in finale but no positioning attempt visible, suggesting stage not prioritized or team lead-out unavailable.
rider_signals.PETILLI Simone: negative: Crashed and abandoned with suspected collarbone, hit by following rider indicating peloton position too risky for domestique.
